error_signature: Unable to sign without access token
title: HMAC-SHA256 Authentication Error in HTTP Request Node
symptom: When attempting to connect to a third-party API using HMAC-SHA256, n8n throws 'Unable to sign without access token'. This prevents successful API authentication.
root_cause: The error occurs because the HTTP Request node requires a valid access token to generate the HMAC signature. Without the access token, the request cannot be signed, leading to authentication failure.
fix_steps: Add an Edit Fields node to your workflow
Insert a Crypto node to generate the HMAC signature
Use the output of the Crypto node in the Authorization field of the HTTP Request node
Ensure the access token is correctly retrieved before the HTTP Request node
agent_instruction: Set up an Edit Fields node followed by a Crypto node to generate the HMAC signature for the HTTP Request node.
